

### Stretch Goals


-  Calendar view (feature branch Calendar-view)
  - [ ] Add a calendar view that shows tasks on a calendar interface based on their due dates. 
  - [ ] Add another button to toggle the view between the default list view and the kanban view.
  - [ ] Add a project filter (based on a dropdown) in both views to only show tasks for a specific project.

- Keep view (feature branch Keep-view; web component, tests, docs)
  - [ ] Add a keep-like view that shows tasks as cards in a flexible grid layout, allowing for easy reordering and categorization.
  - [ ] Projects are listed as tags on the right-hand side of the card in a collapsible view.
  - [ ] Add a markdown body to the schema. Render the markdown body in a markdown viewer in the task card.
  - [ ] Support of editing the markdown body in a textarea that is activated using a long press on the task card.

  - [ ] Update schema to add
        - a body field for markdown content in tasks.  
        - pert chart support for tasks with dependencies and durations and uncertain due dates
        - milestone flag
        - progress tracking fields (e.g. percent complete)
        - bar to show slack time in blue until due date, turning red when overdue or green if already done
  - [ ] **Google-Drive-Integration** Support storage to google drive. 
  - [ ] **Github-Integration** Support storage to github.
  - [ ] **MS OneDrive-Integration** Support storage to MS OneDrive.
  - [ ] **Filter-Update** filters by 
        - date, 
        - project, 
        - importance

- Tests and Documentation
  - [ ] Improve documentation with Mermaid UML charts (class diagrams, sequence diagrams) to illustrate app architecture, component relationships, and data flow.
  - [ ] Access to the QA dashboard via WebMCP to allow agents to run all tests, a test suite, or a single test from chat and get the result, test logs, and screenshots when relevant.

- UI/UX
  - [ ] Add calendar view (web component, tests, docs)
  - [ ] Add gantt view (web component, tests, docs)
  - [ ] Add PERT view (web component, tests, docs)

- PWA
  - [ ] Add service worker integration for offline support, allowing the app to function without an internet connection and sync changes when connectivity is restored.
  - [ ] Implement the [PRPL pattern](https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/prpl-pattern) (Push, Render, Pre-cache, Lazy-load)
  - [ ] Add a web app manifest to provide metadata about the app, such as its name, icons, and theme colors, to enhance the user experience when installing the app on their device.
  - [ ] Add analytics integration to track user interactions and gather insights on how the app is being used
